display.purpose: Hero headline only, one per page.

headline.purpose: Article section headings (h2) and page titles.

title.purpose: Card titles, clamped to two lines.

code.purpose: Inline code and command blocks; the hero artifact.

shadows.name: ambient-low
shadows.purpose: Resting state for solid buttons and small controls.

shadows.name: ambient-high
shadows.purpose: Hero CTA at rest; cards on hover.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Default easing for all state transitions.

motion.name: view-transition
motion.purpose: Astro ClientRouter page transitions with instant fallback.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Terminal Blue fill, white text; deepens on hover. The default action button.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #3b82f6; color: #ffffff; padding: 8px 16px; border: none; border-radius: 6px; font-family: ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; font-size: 16px; font-weight: 500; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,0.05); cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.2s ease-in-out; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #2563eb; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #1d4ed8; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Outline Button
components.description: 2px Terminal Blue border, no fill; hover tints the background. The secondary action.
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { background: #ffffff; color: #2563eb; padding: 8px 16px; border: 2px solid #3b82f6; border-radius: 6px; font-family: ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; font-size: 16px; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.2s ease-in-out, color 0.2s ease-in-out; } .ds-btn-outline:hover { background: #eff6ff; } .ds-btn-outline:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #1d4ed8; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Hero CTA
components.description: The one flamboyant element: blue-to-indigo gradient, lifts and deepens on hover. One per page, always the primary action.
components.html: Browse the guides
components.css: .ds-cta-hero { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; background: linear-gradient(90deg, #3b82f6, #6366f1); color: #ffffff; padding: 16px 32px; border: none; border-radius: 8px; font-family: ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; font-size: 18px; font-weight: 600; text-decoration: none; box-shadow: 0 10px 15px rgba(0,0,0,0.08); cursor: pointer; transition: transform 0.3s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1), box-shadow 0.3s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1), background 0.3s; } .ds-cta-hero:hover { background: linear-gradient(90deg, #2563eb, #4f46e5); transform: scale(1.05); box-shadow: 0 20px 25px rgba(0,0,0,0.12); } .ds-cta-hero:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #1d4ed8; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Tag Chip
components.description: Blue-tint pill linking to a tag page; tint deepens on hover. Never a static ornament.
components.html: docker self-hosting vps
components.css: .ds-chip-tag { display: inline-block; background: #dbeafe; color: #1d4ed8; padding: 4px 8px; border-radius: 9999px; font-family: ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.5; text-decoration: none; transition: background 0.2s ease-in-out; } .ds-chip-tag:hover { background: #bfdbfe; } .ds-chip-tag:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #1d4ed8; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Post Card
components.description: The article index card: flat-ish at rest, lifts to scale 1.02 on hover (the lift-on-intent rule).
components.html:
dockerself-hosting

Install Docker on Ubuntu 24.04: the tested way

Every command run on a fresh VPS before publishing. Copy, paste, done.

Dragos Balota · Jul 2026
components.css: .ds-card-post { background: #ffffff; border-radius: 8px; box-shadow: 0 4px 6px rgba(0,0,0,0.07); padding: 20px; font-family: ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; transition: transform 0.3s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1), box-shadow 0.3s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-card-post:hover { transform: scale(1.02); box-shadow: 0 10px 15px rgba(0,0,0,0.08); } .ds-card-post-tags { display: flex; gap: 8px; margin-bottom: 8px; } .ds-card-post-chip { background: #dbeafe; color: #1d4ed8; padding: 4px 8px; border-radius: 9999px; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; } .ds-card-post-title { margin: 0 0 8px; font-size: 20px; font-weight: 700; line-height: 1.375; } .ds-card-post-title a { color: #111827; text-decoration: none; transition: color 0.2s ease-in-out; } .ds-card-post-title a:hover { color: #2563eb; } .ds-card-post-desc { margin: 0 0 16px; color: #6b7280; font-size: 16px; line-height: 1.625; } .ds-card-post-meta { color: #9ca3af; font-size: 14px; }

components.name: Notice (Info)
components.description: Article callout: tint background with a full 1px border of the same hue; a leading icon marks severity at a glance.
components.html:
Prerequisite

You need a non-root user with sudo privileges before running these commands.
components.css: .ds-notice-info { background: #eff6ff; border: 1px solid #93c5fd; border-radius: 8px; padding: 16px 24px; font-family: ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; color: #111827; } .ds-notice-info-title { display: block; font-size: 16px; font-weight: 600; margin-bottom: 4px; } .ds-notice-info-body { margin: 0; font-size: 16px; line-height: 1.625; }

components.name: Nav Highlight Pill
components.description: The single gradient pill in the sticky header — the nav's primary action, same gesture as the hero CTA.
components.html: ArticlesSeriesFree Tools
components.css: .ds-nav { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; font-family: ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; } .ds-nav-link { color: #6b7280; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; text-decoration: none; padding: 8px 16px; border-radius: 8px; transition: color 0.3s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-nav-link:hover { color: #2563eb; } .ds-nav-pill { background: linear-gradient(90deg, #3b82f6, #6366f1); color: #ffffff; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; text-decoration: none; padding: 8px 16px; border-radius: 8px; box-shadow: 0 4px 6px rgba(0,0,0,0.07); transition: box-shadow 0.3s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1), background 0.3s; } .ds-nav-pill:hover { background: linear-gradient(90deg, #2563eb, #4f46e5); box-shadow: 0 10px 15px rgba(0,0,0,0.08); }

narrative.overview:
Bitdoze is a well-thumbed workshop manual: always open to the right page, every spec tested, grease on the pages. The visual system exists to get a developer from a search result to a working terminal command as fast as possible, and to make the recommendation that follows feel earned. Personality: practical, direct, trustworthy. The reference point for feel is DigitalOcean's tutorial pages — clean, scannable, code-forward, calm.

The system is light-first with a fully designed dark twin (the console palette), not an inverted afterthought. One brand hue — Terminal Blue — carries links, tags, and identity; Indigo Spark appears only beside it on primary calls to action. Code blocks, tables, and step sequences are the hero artifacts; everything decorative yields to them.
keyCharacteristics: Light-first with a designed dark twin (console palette), swapped via CSS variables.
One blue voice; indigo only ever appears next to it, never alone.
Flat by default, lift on intent — shadows and scale answer state, not decoration.
The code block is the most respected element on the page.
No webfonts: the reader's OS renders the page. Speed is a feature.

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Terminal Blue is the only brand hue on any screen. Indigo Spark exists solely inside the blue-to-indigo CTA gradient and the hero accent word; if it appears anywhere else, it's wrong.

rules.name: The Dark Twin Rule
rules.body: Every light token has a console counterpart, and dark mode must be designed with the same care as light — no pure black, no inverted-wash grays, contrast re-verified per pair.

rules.name: The System Stack Rule
rules.body: No webfonts, ever. The reader's OS renders the page; the milliseconds saved are part of the brand promise of respecting the reader's time.

rules.name: The Wash Link Rule
rules.body: Prose links are ink-colored with a translucent blue wash behind the baseline that grows on hover (34% to 54% of the line box). They are never the default browser blue underline.

rules.name: The Lift-on-Intent Rule
rules.body: Shadows and scale appear only as a response to state. If a surface glows, floats, or pulses at rest, it's decoration — remove it.

dos: Do keep Terminal Blue the only brand hue per screen; Indigo Spark appears solely inside the CTA gradient and hero accent (The One Voice Rule).
Do design dark mode from the console palette and re-verify every text/background pair at 4.5:1 or better (WCAG AA).
Do hold body text at Ink (#111827) on Paper, and keep Ink Subtle (#9ca3af) for large or non-essential text only.
Do make every shadow or scale transform answer a state change — hover, focus, active (The Lift-on-Intent Rule).
Do treat code blocks, commands, and configs as the hero artifact: legible, copyable, never truncated without a scroll affordance.
Do keep prose links in the wash style — ink text, translucent blue highlight, growing on hover (The Wash Link Rule).
donts: Don't ship the ad-cluttered tutorial-farm feel: no popups, no interstitial affiliate blocks, no content buried under chrome.
Don't use the generic AI-blog aesthetic: no purple gradients beyond the single blue-to-indigo CTA gesture, no glassmorphism as decoration, no glow effects.
Don't drift into the corporate docs-portal look: no dry gray-on-gray pages stripped of the blue voice and the practitioner tone.
Don't become a Medium/Substack clone: Bitdoze has its own header, its own cards, its own typography rhythm.
Don't add webfonts; the system stack is the brand (The System Stack Rule).
Don't use Indigo Spark alone, on body text, or on large surfaces.
Don't add side stripes, gradient text, or ambient animation beyond the documented lift-on-intent and the hero accent word.
